title: Low Income Housing Help in Cleveland, Beyond Section 8
metaTitle: Low Income Housing Help in Cleveland, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for low income housing help in Cleveland beyond Section 8? See how CMHA vouchers and income limits work, and how to find a voucher-friendly rental home.
answerBox: The main low-income housing resource in Cleveland is the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program run by CMHA (Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority), which subsidizes rent based on household income. Applying is only half the challenge, since Cleveland has no source-of-income protection law; the other half is finding a landlord who accepts vouchers, which is where Rent Finder Cleveland's voucher-friendly portfolio fits in.

sections.h2: What counts as low-income housing help in Cleveland?
body: For most renters in Cleveland, "low-income housing help" means one program above all: the **Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8)** program run by the **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)**, which pays part of a qualifying household's rent directly to a private landlord. It's the primary rental-assistance program covering the county, and most other resources point back to it.

This guide covers how CMHA's voucher program works, who qualifies by income, the practical gap between qualifying for help and actually finding a landlord who accepts it, and where Rent Finder Cleveland fits into that picture.

CMHA accepts preliminary Housing Choice Voucher applications year-round, with no closing date, through its [online applicant portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). Selection isn't strictly first-come, first-served — CMHA pulls names for review through periodic **lottery drawings** from that preliminary list, and reaching your name can take anywhere from a few days to several months.

To apply you'll need an active email address, Social Security numbers and dates of birth for every household member, and household income information. CMHA also keeps computers available at its Main Campus (8120 Kinsman Road) for applicants who need in-person access.

sections.h2: Do you qualify? CMHA income limits
body: Housing Choice Voucher eligibility is based on household size and income, measured against HUD's very-low-income limits for the Cleveland metro area. CMHA's income limits effective **April 3, 2025** looked like this — check [CMHA's site](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) for the current figures before applying, since HUD updates these limits every spring.

sections.h2: The gap between qualifying for a voucher and finding a landlord who'll take it
body: Qualifying for a Housing Choice Voucher solves only half the problem — the other half is finding a landlord willing to rent to a voucher holder. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers as a source of income, and the City of Cleveland itself does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance (its 2024 "Pay to Stay" ordinance dropped that provision during negotiations). A handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs — including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights — do have local voucher-protection ordinances, but that protection does not extend citywide in Cleveland.

In practice, that means voucher holders in the City of Cleveland need to specifically seek out landlords who choose to accept Section 8, rather than assuming every listing will. Our [guide to source-of-income rules in Cleveland](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) covers this patchwork in more detail.

sections.h2: Where Rent Finder Cleveland fits in
body: This is where we can help directly: every one of the 90+ rental homes we manage across Greater Cleveland accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, with no exceptions. Our homes are concentrated in East and Southeast Cleveland neighborhoods including Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker, with a smaller number on the West side.

If you have a voucher in hand — or are waiting on CMHA — [tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll show you what's currently available. If your need is more urgent than a standard application timeline, see how [emergency housing options](/cleveland-rentals/more/emergency-housing-cleveland-same-day/) differ from a voucher.

faq.q: What is the main low-income housing program in Cleveland?
faq.a: The Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program, administered by the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), is the primary rental-assistance program covering the City of Cleveland and the rest of Cuyahoga County. It pays a portion of a qualifying household's rent directly to a participating landlord.

faq.q: How do I apply for a CMHA Housing Choice Voucher?
faq.a: Apply through CMHA's online applicant portal, which accepts preliminary applications year-round. You'll need an active email address, Social Security numbers and birth dates for household members, and income information. CMHA then selects applicants for review through periodic lottery drawings rather than strict first-come, first-served order.

faq.q: What income qualifies for a Housing Choice Voucher in Cuyahoga County?
faq.a: Eligibility is based on HUD's very-low-income limits for the metro area, which CMHA set effective April 3, 2025 — for example, $34,800 for one person and $49,700 for a four-person household. HUD updates these limits annually, so check CMHA's current chart before applying.

faq.q: Does having a voucher guarantee I can rent anywhere in Cleveland?
faq.a: No. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance, though several suburbs do. Voucher holders generally need to find landlords who specifically choose to accept Section 8.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland accept Housing Choice Vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home in our 90+ unit Cleveland-area portfolio accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready. Tell us your voucher status, bedroom needs, and preferred area and we'll show you what's currently available.
